steps: Sign in to your 1Password account (requires a 1Password Teams or Business plan)
Give your Connect server a name and select which vaults it can access
Download the 1password-credentials.json file — this is used to run the Connect server
Click 'Issue Token' and give the token a name and set an expiration (30, 90, or 180 days)
Copy the token immediately — it is shown only once
Deploy the 1Password Connect Server using Docker with the credentials file (see https://developer.1password.com/docs/connect/get-started/)

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Connect Server Token
credential_fields.description: The Bearer token issued for your 1Password Connect server. Used in the Authorization header.

notes: Requires a 1Password Teams or Business plan — not available on personal accounts
The Connect server must be self-hosted (Docker); the token authenticates against your self-hosted instance
The base URL of this API is your own Connect server URL, not a public Anthropic endpoint
Tokens expire after 30, 90, or 180 days and must be rotated
The token is shown only once at creation time — save it securely

test_endpoint.description: List all vaults accessible to the Connect server token
